text: Hi, my name is Scott Chaney. I am a urologist at the Mayo Clinic in Phoenix, Arizona.

Today we're going to be doing a case of a partial nephrectomy for a renal tumor in a forty-nine-year-old obese man with a five-centimeter posterior tumor. The case is made a little bit more difficult by the fact that his BMI is forty-three and the tumor is located very posteriorly, but we plan to free up the kidney, flip it over so that we can see the backside of the kidney, and do the partial nephrectomy. I plan to do the case off clamp without clamping the vessels to the kidney, but I always have that option available for me if the bleeding gets intense. So we're going to see how this goes.
